client_input.title: Sita Soren vs Union of India
client_input.description: The case revolves around the interpretation of Article 105(2) and Article 194(2) of the Constitution, specifically whether a legislator who accepts a bribe to cast a vote in a certain direction or speak about certain issues is protected by parliamentary privilege. The court must decide whether the immunity under these articles extends to criminal prosecution for bribery.

citations_found.ai_summary: The dispute in this Indian legal case, Sri C T Ravi vs State By Bagewadi P S, revolves around a complaint filed by a woman Member of the Legislative Assembly against the petitioner, a Member of the Legislative Council, alleging that he made utterances that undermined her dignity. The Karnataka High Court decided on the matter, which involved the petitioner seeking to quash the registration of the crime. The court's decision was pronounced on May 2, 2025, after reserving its order on February 24, 2025, although the exact outcome of the decision is not specified in the provided text.

Narsimha Rao vs Central Bureau Of Investigation on 12 September, 1997 Equivalent citations: 1997VAD 265, 68 DLT553, 1997 DRJ108, 1...
citations_found.ai_summary: The dispute in this Indian legal case, P.V. Narsimha Rao vs Central Bureau Of Investigation, revolves around allegations of bribery and corruption involving the former Prime Minister of India, P.V. Narsimha Rao, and his associates, who were accused of paying money to Members of Parliament to secure their support and avoid a no-confidence motion in 1993. The court decided that there was sufficient evidence to frame charges against Narsimha Rao and others, rejecting their petitions to dismiss the charges. The court's decision was based on the evidence presented by the Central Bureau of Investigation, which alleged that Narsimha Rao and his associates had used money to influence the votes of MPs and secure their support.
